title: Green Light for Scale Green Energy to Deploy Six Hydrogen Refuelling Stations in Spain
content: Scale Green Energy, a subsidiary of EnagÃ¡s, has secured over â‚¬8 million from the EUâ€™s Connecting Europe Facility (CEF-AFIF) to construct six renewable hydrogen refuelling stations across Spain as part of the ECOhynet project. These stations, each with a 1,000 kg/day capacity and operating at 350 and 700 bar, will serve both light and heavy-duty vehicles along the Atlantic and Mediterranean corridors of the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T). The total investment for the project exceeds â‚¬26.7 million. This funding complements the â‚¬75.8 million previously awarded to EnagÃ¡s for studies on Spainâ€™s Hydrogen Backbone Network and the H2Med corridor, including the CelZa and BarMar projects. The effort supports the EUâ€™s ReFuelEU goals for alternative fuel infrastructure development.
author: Analysts Account
